intended_use: MSKai is an image identification, post-processing, measurement, and reporting software tool that provides qualitative viewing and quantitative spine measurements from previously-acquired T2 weighted D
intended_use_full: MSKai is an image identification, post-processing, measurement, and reporting software tool that provides qualitative viewing and quantitative spine measurements from previously-acquired T2 weighted DICOM lumbar spine Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI) images for users' review, evaluation and analysis. It provides the following functionality to assist users in identifying, observing, measuring and reporting measurements: Anatomy segmentation; Anatomy labeling; Anatomy measurement; and Export of measurement results to a qualitative and quantitative report for user's evaluation, amendment and authorization
target_condition: Lumbar spine anatomy evaluation and measurement; may assist in assessment of conditions affecting the lumbar spine such as disc protrusion, spondylolisthesis, and spinal stenosis
ground_truth: Ground truth defined by 3 neurosurgeons, 1 interventional radiologist, and 1 PhD in Biomechanics on segmentations and measurements; measurements also analyzed by 4 independent experts (2 neurosurgeons, 2 radiologists); inter-rater reliability conducted among experts
other_metrics: Mean Dice Coefficient (MDC) for segmentation: range 0.911-0.993 (all ≥0.8 limit); Mean Absolute Error (MAE) for measurements: protruding disc material 1.19-1.23mm (≤2mm limit), intervertebral angle 2.4-2.9° (≤6° limit), vertebral body height 0.64-0.97mm (≤2mm limit), disc height 0.87-0.93mm (≤2mm limit), spondylolisthesis 1.01-1.08mm (≤2mm limit), lordotic angle 2.99° (≤6° limit), dural sac diameter 1.3mm (≤2mm limit)
